architecture_fit.note: Exactly the goal's spec (2L/4H/d64 attention-only, minimal delta from base_model); best head shows positive excess 0.127 with 89% of positions positive, so the Viterbi predecessor signature is real if modest.

baseline_comparison.note: Uniform (~0) and random (-0.001) baselines are reported under identical conditions and clearly beaten, but these come free from the shared task; no attempt-authored strawman (untrained model, ablated positional encoding).

faithfulness.note: Purely correlational attention measurement — no ablation or activation patching to show the model actually uses this head, and the README neither acknowledges the gap nor proposes such a check despite it being a trained model.

operating_range.note: Only the canonical seq_len=20/seed=42 condition; no variation across sequence length, scale, or ablation seeds — the per-position breakdown shows within-sequence structure but not the required input-scale axis.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: N/A — explicitly a trained attempt with no hand-set circuit.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is broken as shipped: main.py never writes payload.json or attn_weights.npy, so app.py's load_payload returns empty and every plot renders None and the metrics box shows '{}'.

visualisation_rationale.note: README's 'Why this visualisation' describes a text/table summary, but app.py actually implements bar/line/heatmap plots — the stated rationale contradicts the implementation and isn't compelling.

notes: A correctly-specified 2L/4H attention-only transformer that genuinely exhibits the Viterbi predecessor signature (best head excess 0.127, 89% of positions positive, cleanly beating uniform and random baselines). However it stops at a correlational attention measurement: no causal/ablation evidence that the model uses this head, no operating-range sweep beyond the single canonical condition, and the Demo viz is broken because main.py never saves the payload.json/attn_weights.npy that app.py loads. Mean of scored items ~2.5 → borderline.
